title: ruellia pedunculata subsp. pinetorum
intro: Ruellia pedunculata subsp. pinetorum, also known as Ruellia pinetorum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain flavonoids and terpenoids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Ruellia pinetorum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various ailments. Herbal preparations of Ruellia pinetorum often involve the leaves being made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
